title: Incredibly Jammy Ukrainian Potato Dumplings
description: A traditional Ukrainian dish featuring coriander and score-fat, now made jain-friendly, these dumplings are a crowd-pleaser with their jammy and aerated texture. They are perfect for a dinner gathering.
instructions: Boil the grated potatoes in salted water until they are slightly tender.
Drain the potatoes and let them cool.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooled potatoes, flour, coriander seeds, score-fat, onion, salt, and black pepper.
Mix the ingredients together until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for about 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into small balls and flatten each ball slightly into a disk shape.
Heat a non-stick pan with a little water over medium heat and cook the dumplings for about 5 minutes on each side.
Serve the dumplings hot with your favorite jam or sauce.
Traditionally, these dumplings are served with a side of fried onions and sour cream, but feel free to get creative with your toppings.
